design_philosophy.core_loop: Climb vertically through platforming stages, avoid enemies, reach objectives (key or locks) to progress to next stage at increased difficulty
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Vertical navigation under time pressure with enemies spawning dynamically; bonus timer creates continuous scoring tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as underdog hero rescuing father from captor; role reversal from original Donkey Kong establishes emotional narrative hook
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits to vertical climbing as core mechanic (vines/chains/ropes) rather than horizontal platforming; enemy knockback via fruit creates distinct interaction model; difficulty curve resets player through same stages at escalating challenge rather than entirely new content

difficulty.lives_system: Player loses a life on enemy contact, projectile hit, excessive fall distance, water/bottom-screen fall, or bonus timer expiration; game ends when all lives exhausted
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints within stages; failure returns player to stage start
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty increases automatically through stage repetition with escalating enemy behavior; unused code suggests original design considered difficulty modulation based on player proximity to objectives, but this was not implemented in final version

progression.unlock_mechanism: Four distinct stages unlock sequentially (1→2→3→4); after stage 4 completion, player loops back to stage 1 at increased difficulty; kill screen occurs at level 22 due to integer overflow in level counter
replayability_hooks: Bonus timer scoring incentivizes speed-running through familiar stages
Enemy behavior escalation provides mechanical challenge progression
Stage order varies by territory (JP: 1-2-3-4 repeat; NA: 1-4, 1-2-4, 1-3-4, 1-2-3-4, then 1-2-3-4)

world_building_techniques: Opening cutscene establishes role reversal: Mario as villain, DK Jr. as rescuer
Environmental progression reflects narrative (forest cage → helicopter transport → hideout with distributed keys → final chains)
Stage-end cutscenes show Mario fleeing cage or DK Jr. progressing toward father
Visual level themes (forest, hideout locations) support story of pursuit and rescue

boss_design.philosophy: No traditional boss encounters; Mario functions as persistent antagonist/environmental hazard rather than contained boss fight; stage completion is determined by key/lock objectives rather than direct confrontation

faction_indicators: DK Jr. faction: underdog hero, rescuer archetype, filial duty
Mario faction: captor/oppressor, technological advantage (cage mechanics), pursuit persistence
danger_signals: Nitpickers dropping eggs represent unpredictable environmental hazards
Sparks on wiring suggest electrified defenses
Bonus timer depletion as failure mechanic creates urgency/desperation narrative
environmental_storytelling: Cage with DK inside establishes imprisonment visually
Distributed keys across hideout show security/containment measures
Vertical stage design reflects journey from ground to cage/rescue point
Fruit as hazard removal tool creates asymmetric interaction between rescue attempt and defense systems

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor: Donkey Kong Jr. NES/FDS article)
